observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
1.11.1. Municipal Treasurer –
a) Record in her cashbook Cash in Bank Trust Fund (Proper)
the adjustments presented in item 1.6 except no. 4 addition
to reflect the correct balance of the cashbook; and
b) Municipal Treasurer observe careful and timely recording of
the transactions in the books to avoid errors.
1.11.2. Municipal Accountant –
a) Trace the sources of the outstanding differences of the Cash
Local Treasury and Cash Advances observed and correct
errors, as necessary; and
b) Prepare quarterly reconciliation statements of Cash Local
Treasury and Cash Advances and monthly reconciliation
statements of Cash in Bank using the actual balance of the
cashbooks.

2.19. We recommended that the:
2.19.1. Municipal Accountant –
a) Immediately reconcile the Cash Local Treasury balance per
accounting records with the cashbook balances to account
for the cash on hand necessary for deposit; and
b) Record receipts of trust collections in the CRJ under the TF
books.
2.19.2. Municipal Treasurer –
a) Record adjustments in the Cash Local Treasury cashbooks
for the errors identified in item 2.8;
b) Deposit immediately the correct amount of remaining
collections on hand based on reconciliation with the
Municipal Accountant;
c) Maintain separate set of accountable form 51-C (Official
Receipts) and cashbook for TF solely used for all receipts
of the Municipality under a trust agreement for use of
specific purposes and record and prepare separate RCDs
and RAAFs for said collections; and
d) Regularly update her cashbooks and submit RCDs for
timely reconciliation with the accounting records; and
deposit collections daily and intact.

3.13. We recommended and Management agreed that:
3.13.1. The Municipal Accountant prepare Quarterly Report on the Status
of Unliquidated Cash Advances and Other Receivables and
submit the same regularly to the Audit Team and maintain
subsidiary ledgers;
3.13.2. Management cause immediate collection of the outstanding
receivables from responsible officers and employees or debtors
to settle the accounts; and
3.13.3. In case a request for write-off is applicable, Management follow
the guidelines set forth under COA Circular 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016 supported by the supplemental guidelines
under COA Circular No. 2022-01 dated January 24, 2022.

4.21. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
4.21.1. Head of the Inventory Committee to initiate the conduct of
complete and annual physical count of PPE and local road
networks, and subsequent preparation and submission of the
RPCPPE and RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation;
4.21.2. Municipal Treasurer/GSO maintain updated Property Cards per
category of PPE;
4.21.3. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to locate the
records to complete the details and specifications of the roads
and their components to facilitate in the preparation and
maintenance of required records and reports for all roads and
their components – Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads,
Subsidiary Ledgers, Local Road Network Property Cards, Local
Road Inventory, Road Map, Report on Local Road Network and
year-end RPCLRN; and
4.21.4. Municipal Accountant (a) maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards,
(b) reconcile ledger cards with the property cards and RPCPPE
and verify variances for necessary and reasonable adjustments,
and (c) complete the recognition as PPE of the roads that are
still in the Registry of Public Infrastructures/ closed to the
Government Equity from prior to 2016.

5.11. We recommended and Management agreed to:
5.11.1. Adjust the Inventory accounts to fairly present the financial
statements based on the proposed adjusting journal entries in
item 5.10;
5.11.2. Observe the prescribed accounting guidelines in recording new
properties either as PPE, semi-expendable or expense outright;
and
5.11.3. Conduct physical count, maintain subsidiary ledger, and prepare
Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for future
procurement and maintenance of inventories on hand.

6.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
6.16.1. The Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the mentioned
documents for the payment of salaries and wages;
6.16.2. The Human Resource and Management Officer require immediate
submission of DTRs and relevant supporting documents from all
employees after end of each month;
6.16.3. The Municipal Treasurer (a) only pay salaries of employees with
complete documents from the Human Resource and
Management Officer and refund all unclaimed; and (b) submit
Report of Cash Disbursements, liquidation reports and
supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant within
reglementary period; and
6.16.4. The Municipal Accountant (a) allow succeeding cash advances
only when the previous cash advance was liquidated and demand
immediate liquidation or refund of all due for liquidation; and (b)
submit the Report of Cash Disbursements, liquidation reports
and supporting documents for audit within ten days of the
ensuing month.

7.7. We recommended that Management trace back from municipal records
the source of the balances and request ledgers/records from the GSIS
for previous years to identify the mistakes or variances, reconcile with
the said agency and correctly report the balances of the account Due to
GSIS.

8.7. We recommended that Management:
8.7.1. Trace back source of abnormal balances and Request
ledgers/records from the HDMF/Pag-IBIG from years 2010/2012 to
identify the unpaid loans or contributions of employees annually;
and
8.7.2. Exert effort to locate Municipal records and trace accounting
entries and treasury records for withholdings and remittances and
request from employee records of their loans and reconcile the
same with the records obtained from the concerned agencies and
appropriate adjustments or reconciliation.

9.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain updated
records of general and subsidiary ledgers and reconcile monthly the two
records to ascertain the balances presented in the Financial Statements
and provide adequate and reliable information/details of the accounts.

10.22. We recommended that the:
10.22.1. Municipal Accountant submit immediately the noted lacking
documents to establish the validity of the disbursements;
10.22.2. Municipal Treasurer/GSO/Inspection Officer use proper forms – IAR,
for the receipt and inspection of supplies and materials
delivered; PAR – for issuances of equipment/property to
establish accountability;
10.22.3. Municipal Mayor regulate the use of government vehicles
through:
▪ Use of the Requisition and Issue Voucher for issuances of
fuel;
▪ Municipal Treasurer/GSO maintain list of all municipal
vehicles and prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption
of government motor transportation; and
▪ preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travels; and
10.22.4. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims
are supported with sufficient, relevant, and completely
accomplished documents prior submission to the Municipal
Treasurer for payments to claimant/supplier; and the
Municipal Treasurer process only claims that are duly certified
as complete.

11.12. We recommended and Management agreed that:
11.12.1. Personnel concerned submit immediately the
reports/documents enumerated in paragraph 14.6 of this
observation; and
11.12.2. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor ensure timely submission
from offices concerned of documents, records and reports to
the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes, enforce
suspension of salaries for non-submission and provide
additional accounting staff.

12.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the members of the
BAC submit to the Municipal Accountant copies of perfected contracts
and purchase orders and consequently the Municipal Accountant
submit these to the Audit Team within the reglementary period of five
days.

13.8. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
13.8.1. Require the concerned personnel to immediately submit the (a)
list of all implemented and on-going projects of the Municipality
for CY 2023 and (b) proof of postings of signboards and/or
tarpaulins, if any; and
13.8.2. Henceforth, ensure adherence to the transparency and
accountability provisions of the National Government as
provided by the above-mentioned rules and regulations,
submission of the required lists and proper notification to the
Audit Team of postings of signboards and/or tarpaulins of all
projects, programs, and activities.

14.32. Considering the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended and
Management agreed that the:
14.32.1. Members of the BAC –
➢ Submit the lacking technical documents and eligibility
requirements as enumerated in the attached Annex, and
the signed and notarized contracts discussed above;
➢ Submit the minutes of meeting and attendance for the pre-
procurement and pre-bid conferences for the contracts
noted, if such were conducted, as well as, the certification
for the posting requirements if complied;
➢ Henceforth, require contractors to indicate the date of their
receipt of the NTP to serve as the effectivity date of the
contract and basis in computing liquidated damages, in
case of delay by the contractor in the performance of the
contract;
➢ Ensure that invitations in writing are sent to observers at
least five days before the conduct of the required
procurement activity and attach the duly received invitation
letters as part of the contract and bidding documents for
the contract; and
➢ Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in the Updated
2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, including latest
applicable Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB)
issuances in the conduct of public bidding for government
procurements to enhance transparency and assure that
projects are legally awarded to the most favorable and
qualified bidders; and
➢ Ensure submitted documents were complete in form and
details (e.g. dates and signature).
14.32.2. Municipal Mayor direct the Members of the BAC, BAC
Secretariat and BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to
attend webinars/seminars on the 2016 Revised IRR of the
procurement law and its updates.
14.32.3. Management specially Members of the BAC to ensure that the
6th Edition of PBDs will be in used in the succeeding years on
the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects.

15.13. We recommended and Management agreed that the Members of the
BAC, through the Municipal Accountant, submit immediately the
complete bidding and contract documents, in addition to the items
identified in item 15.2, relative to the procurement of farms inputs and
farm agricultural machineries.

16.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat
to submit immediately the approved CY 2023 APP and supplemental, if
any and ensure regular submission of the same including its supporting
PPMPs to COA for audit purposes.

17.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately require all
concerned personnel to prepare a status on the actions taken on audit
findings and recommendations in the CY 2022 AAR (part II and part III)
as of year-end and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for
consolidation through the AAPSI form and consequently be submitted
to the Audit Team on or before April 5, 2024; and henceforth, ensure
timely submission of the duly accomplished AAPSI within 60 days from
receipt of COA AAR and after end of each year.

19.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
19.6.1. Municipal Mayor as chairman of the LSB ensure approval of
annual SEF budget; and
19.6.2. Public School District Supervisor as Co-Chairman of the LSB
prepare annual SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1/Income and
Expenditure Estimate in accordance with the guidelines,
supported with the Local Development Investment Program
(LDIP) and Annual Investment Program.

22.26. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
22.26.1. Direct immediate submission of the CY 2023 revised or
supplemental LDRRMFIP duly approved by the Sangguniang
Bayan Members, if any;
22.26.2. Ensure LDRRMF Appropriation of not less than 5% of
estimated total revenues from all sources and duly supported
with correctly prepared and reviewed LDRRMFIP.
22.26.3. Require concerned personnel to prepare and submit timely
monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF,
RAAO, subsidiary ledger and List of
Obligations/Expenditures with supporting schedules; and
22.26.4. Ensure that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from
prior years be included as a separate caption under the
current year’s LDRRMFIP or supplemental LDRRMFIP duly
approved by the Sangguniang Bayan mainly for DRRM
activities in compliance with the above-cited provisions.

1.24. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1.24.1. Head of the Inventory Committee initiate the conduct of complete
and annual physical count of PPE and local road networks, and
subsequent preparation and submission of the RPCPPE and
RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation;
1.24.2. Municipal Treasurer/GSO (a) issue ARE/PAR for all issuances of
equipment to users for establishing accountability, and (b)
maintain updated Property Cards per category of PPE;
1.24.3. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant locate the records to
complete the details and specifications of the roads and their
components to facilitate in the preparation and maintenance of
required records and reports for all roads and their components –
Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, Subsidiary Ledgers,
Local Road Network Property Cards, Local Road Inventory, Road
Map, Report on Local Road Network and year-end RPCLRN; and
1.24.4. Municipal Accountant (a) maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards, (b)
reconcile ledger cards with the property cards and RPCPPE and
verify variances for necessary and reasonable adjustments, (c)
complete the recognition as PPE of the roads that are still in the
Registry of Public Infrastructures/ closed to the Government Equity
from prior to 2016, and (d) provide depreciation for all depreciable
Infrastructure Assets to establish proper valuation of PPE
accounts.

2.6. We recommended and Management agreed to:
2.6.1. Request ledgers/records from the Landbank of the Philippines,
ADTEMPCO and HDMF/Pag-IBIG from years 2010/2012 to identify
the unpaid loans or contributions of employees annually; and
2.6.2. Exert effort to locate Municipal records and trace accounting
entries and treasury records for withholdings and remittances and
request from employee records of their loans and reconcile the
same with the records obtained from the concerned agencies and
appropriate adjustments or reconciliation.

3.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant:
3.9.1. Prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the proposed
accounting adjustment in paragraph 3.8; and
3.9.2. Henceforth, recognize rent income from the leased property using
the accrual basis of accounting and fully disclose information
relevant to the rent income account in the Notes to Financial
Statement.

4.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant prepare the JEV to effect the proposed accounting
adjustments in paragraph 4.5; and henceforth, observe proper
accounting for liquidation of grants, donations and subsidies to the
Municipality and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers.

7.5. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
7.5.1. Municipal Treasurer exhaust all remedies and means to
immediately collect the outstanding balance of Globe Telecom, Inc.
send monthly notice of collection or billing to Globe Telecom, Inc.
for the current rental due and the remaining unpaid rentals from
previous periods, if any; and
7.5.2. Municipal Accountant maintain SLs for receivable accounts per
debtor and support entries in the journals with JEV attached with
the Payment Advice and certified copies of the official receipt and
check payment for the collected rent.

11.6. We recommended that Management immediately submit the list of all
implemented and on-going projects of the Municipality for CY 2021 and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency and accountability provisions of
the National Government as provided by the above-mentioned rules and
regulations.

15.27. In light of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended and
Management agreed that the:
15.27.1. Members of the BAC –
 Submit the lacking technical documents and eligibility
requirements as enumerated in the attached Annex;
 Submit the minutes of meeting and attendance for the pre-
procurement and pre-bid conferences for the contracts
noted, if such were conducted;
 Henceforth, require contractors to indicate the date of their
receipt of the NTP to serve as the effectivity date of the
contract and basis in computing liquidated damages, in
case of delay by the contractor in the performance of the
contract;
 Ensure that invitations in writing are sent to observers at
least five days before the conduct of the required
procurement activity and attach the duly received invitation
letters as part of the contract and bidding documents for
the contract; and
 Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in the Updated
2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, including latest
applicable Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB)
issuances in the conduct of public bidding for government
procurements to enhance transparency and assure that
projects are legally awarded to the most favorable and
qualified bidders;
15.27.2. Municipal Mayor direct the Members of the BAC, BAC
Secretariat and BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to attend
webinars/seminars on the 2016 Revised IRR of the
procurement law and its updates.

16.10. We recommended and Management agreed to:
16.10.1. Submit immediately copy of MOA and SB Resolution for the
above-mentioned fund transfer; and
16.10.2. Henceforth, fully support all transactions which involve funds
from other government agencies with a MOA or any
appropriate document and SB Resolution authorizing the
Municipal Mayor to enter into such agreement and approving
the proposed project and issue official receipts for every fund
transfer received by the Municipality.

17.4. We re commend and Management agreed that the:
17.4.1. Municipal Treasurer transfer the total amount of ₱25,801,162.00
from depository bank of the General Fund (proper) Account to
the depository bank of SAGF - RA 7171 to ensure utilization in
accordance with their specific purposes; and
17.4.2. Municipal Accountant effect the suggested entries under
paragraph 17.3 to fairly present the financial statements of the
General Fund (proper) and the SAGF – RA 7171.

18.4. We recommended and Management agreed that the concerned
personnel prepare and submit timely monthly Reports on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF with supporting schedules.

19.7. We recommended and Management agreed that charges from the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be included in the current year’s
LDRRMFIP or duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan Council and
the Municipal Mayor mainly for DRRM activities in compliance with the
above-cited provisions.

20.5. We recommended that the:
20.5.1. Municipal Treasurer deposit the unspent LDRRMF as of
December 31, 2021 amounting ₱103,448.13 to properly account
the fund and avoid possible mismanagement of the fund;
20.5.2. Municipal Accountant record the journal entries to transfer the
unexpended LDRRMF from the GF to the TF upon deposit of the
unspent LDRRMF to the special trust fund account to enable
proper monitoring; and
20.5.3. Municipal Accountant prepare and present the composition of
the balances under Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund account; henceforth, prepare and
maintain subsidiary ledgers for the mentioned account.

21.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant incorporate
disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements pertinent to the
appropriation, grant and utilization of the LDRRMF including cash and
in-kind donations due to calamities to provide more informative
financial statements.

22.7. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Budget
Officer submit the Sanguniang Bayan Resolution approving the
realignment and amended LDRRMFIP for the procured motor vehicles.

23.8. We recommended and Management agreed to:
23.8.1. Present the Authorization to Purchase Motor Vehicle - five
rescue vehicles (mini-cab/multi-cab) by the DILG-CAR Region
Director to the Municipality of Daguioman and submit a certified
true copy, if any; and
23.8.2. Henceforth, review the current guidelines on the procurement of
motor vehicles for guidance on further procurements.

24.10. In view of the observation above, we recommended that the:
24.10.1. Municipal Accountant (a) draw the necessary journal entry
mentioned in paragraph 24.4, (b) maintain SL for Trust
Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund - cash
donations per donor per purpose for ₱100,000.00 and above per
donor per purpose for ₱100,000.00 and above and as "Various
Donors" per purpose for below ₱100,000.00, and (c) prepare monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with complete funds and
details;
24.10.2. Municipal Treasurer transfer the cash donation from DOE to
the separate bank account for LDRRMF;
24.10.3. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant prepare
monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash
Donations and submit the same to oversight agencies and COA;
24.10.4. Henceforth, Management carefully review agreements and
supporting documents for all cash donations or fund transfers to
properly treat the same in accordance with applicable guidelines,
and adhere to the purpose and terms of agreement for full
attainment of the objective of the funds.

25.5. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
25.5.1. Municipal Mayor ensure the organization and functionality of the
LCPC, including reorganization in accordance with DILG MC No.
2021-039 dated April 7, 2021 and creation of LCPC Inter-agency
Monitoring Task Force (IMTF) to monitor and evaluate the
functionality of the LCPC;
25.5.2. LCPC formulate and develop plans and programs geared towards
the promotion of rights and best interests of children as identified in
Item 5.3.2 of DILG MC No. 2021-039 dated April 7, 2021 and
consequently prepare Annual Work Financial Plan (AWFP) for
inclusion in the local development plan and annual budget; and
25.5.3. LCPC monitor and review implementation of the approved plans
and programs under the LCPC Fund and prepare Accomplishment
Report.

26.7. We recommended that the:
26.7.1. Municipal Mayor as chairman of the LSB ensure approval of
annual SEF budget; and
26.7.2. Public School District Supervisor as Co-Chairman of the LSB
prepare annual SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1/Income and
Expenditure Estimate in accordance with the guidelines.

27.4. We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) identify
GAD programs and activities through the use of HGDG tool/checklist
which would be the basis in the budget determination embodied in the
GBP depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD
interventions.

29.5. We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the
projects.

30.7. We recommended and Management agreed to prepare a well-designed
LDRRMFIP to include priority PPAs, ensure an approved revision of the
LDRRMFIP when necessary and ensure the PPAs’ full and proper
implementation.

31.8. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
ensure implementation of the DILG-endorsed GPB by the lead or
responsible offices and require the GFPS to strengthen the M&E
system to improve utilization and promote gender equality in the
locality.

32.6. We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that planned
PPAs to cater the needs and concerns of the elderly and disabled within
the locality are implemented.

33.8. In view of this, we recommended that:
33.8.1. The Municipal Mayor accelerate the implementation of the above
noted PPAs and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator closely monitor and evaluate the status of
implementation of the subject-PPAs; and
33.8.2. Henceforth, all concerned responsible offices implement the
PPAs in accordance with the approved time-frame in the AIP.

34.6. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
34.6.1. Municipal Treasurer remit to BIR the withheld taxes which
remained outstanding to date (refer to paragraph 34.3);
34.6.2. Municipal Accountant prepare JEV for the recording of
adjustments relative to the unrecorded withholdings;
34.6.3. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary
ledgers for BIR to monitor unremitted withholdings, the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile
monthly their records on taxes withheld and remitted and
immediately remit to BIR any unremitted taxes and adjust
accordingly based on the results of the reconciliation.

35.3. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to BIR
RMO No. 23-2014 on the withholding of applicable taxes on payments to
suppliers/contractors.

observations.managementAction: There was no physical inventory conducted for CY 2020, but the Municipal Engineer will try to meet the required inventory in the succeeding years. Also noted that there are provincial roads being maintained by the LGU and need to be coordinated with the Provincial Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the above-mentioned rules and regulations and immediately submit the list of all implemented and on-going projects of the Municipality for CY 2020 onwards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the limit of reimbursements to traveling and training expenses upon receipt of the audit findings from the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management justify the noted lapses, and henceforth, comply with the PS limitation of 55% in the budget preparation and execution for Personal Services expenditures in adherence to the above-cited provision.
observations.managementAction: Management acknowledged the noted observation in audit and explained that they are trying to limit their personnel services to comply with the limitation set.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and stop the practice of reimbursements, instead, resort to direct payments to suppliers and/or creditors to provide reasonable assurance that the transaction is regular and for the purpose of withholding taxes due to the Government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has mandated to limit reimbursements to traveling and training expenses upon receipt of the audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer to cause the immediate preparation, maintenance and submission of the required registries and the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances for the proper evaluation of the SCBAA for the year under audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer acknowledged the observation and said that he will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the SCABAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board submit the SEF budget and its utilization report for CY 2020, if any, for the evaluation of charges made therefrom; and henceforth, prepare the SEF budget in the form prescribed and within the time frame set and furnish this Office a copy of the Annual SEF Budget, for evaluation of the fund in adherence to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The municipal management noted the recommendations for compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit justification / explanation on the noted deficiencies; and henceforth, conduct pre-bid conference for procurement with ABC of ₱1,000,000.00 or above.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is trying to comply on the requirements of the procurement law and looking for trainings to update themselves of the changes in the IRR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in the required stages of the procurement process in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We recommended that management provide in the Notes to Financial Statements all the required disclosures as mandated under COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management require the contractors to post additional warranty security for those which are still within the 1-year defects liability period. Henceforth, oblige all contractors to post the required warranty securities, else, the 10% retention money should not be released.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020 projects, Management did not require warranty securities. No collection enforced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the implementation of the SWMP is integrated in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised;
 The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects; and
 Revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP
observations.managementAction: The LGU approved the SWMP on December 17, 2019. The SWMP was not yet fully implemented during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management justify the purchase of the dump truck without appropriation. Moreover, we recommended that management provide the audit team with the contract and supporting documents of the procured dump truck for proper review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: No action informed. The noted documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1.4.1 Record the entry to reclassify the completed portions of the
Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets to the account
Road Networks as substantiated by their Certificate of
Completion; and
1.4.2 Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers for each project in the
account Construction in Progress to enable monitoring and
eventual reclassification to the corresponding asset accounts upon
completion.

2.14 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
2.14.1 Head of the Inventory Committee initiate the conduct of complete
and annual physical count of PPE and subsequent preparation
and submission of the RPCPPE to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation;
2.14.2 Municipal Treasurer as General Services Officer maintain
updated Property Cards per category of PPE; and reconcile the
same with the RPCPPE; and
2.14.3 Municipal Accountant maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; and
reconcile the same with the RPCPPE and verify variances for
necessary and reasonable adjustments.

4.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable Infrastructure
Assets as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to establish proper
valuation of PPE accounts.

5.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
5.4.1 Head of the Inventory Committee initiate the conduct inventory of
real properties of the Municipality and prepare RPCPPE and
furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant;
5.4.2 GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintain Property Cards for Land and
keep in custody the Tax Declaration (Owner’s copy) and Original
Certificate of Title and Transfer Certificate of Title;
5.4.3 Municipal Accountant maintain updated PPELC for Land and
reconcile the same with the Original Certificate of Title and
Transfer Certificate of Title, Property Cards for land and
RPCPPE to establish the balance in the books; and
5.4.4 Municipal Assessor register all real estate owned by the
Municipality of Daguioman, Abra, under the Torrens Title System
pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386.

12.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned
officers/employees to provide the Municipal Accountant with the
necessary documents to support the JEVs made in the General Journal
and submit the same to COA for proper evaluation.

20.7 We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses, and
henceforth, comply with the PS limitation of 55% in the budget
preparation and execution for Personal Services expenditures in
adherence to the above-cited provision.

22.8 We have recommended that Management adhere to the transparency
provisions as provided by the above-mentioned rules and regulations and
immediately submit the list of all implemented and on-going projects of
the Municipality for CY 2020 onwards.

27.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned
employee to prepare and submit the required quarterly reports on the
fund utilization and status of implementation of various
programs/projects charged to the Municipal Share from Tobacco Excise
Tax for proper evaluation.

any, for the evaluation of charges made therefrom; and
30.4.2 Henceforth, prepare the SEF budget in the form prescribed and
within the time frame set and furnish this Office a copy of the
Annual SEF Budget, for evaluation of the fund in adherence to
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

31.4 We have recommended that Management prepare and submit to the
Audit Team Annual Utilization Report for the 20% Development Fund
with supporting schedules.

32.11 We have recommended that the:
32.11.1 Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the
lacking supporting documents identified in paragraphs 32.2, 32.5
and 32.9; and
32.11.2 Municipal Mayor lead in the design of the necessary measures
on the process and approval of disbursements to avoid
recurrence of this type of observation and strict adherence to
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the
documentary requirements.

33.4 We have recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with supporting schedules.

34.5 We have recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer and Budget
Officer ensure that an LDRRMFIP is duly prepared and approved by the
Sangguniang Bayan Council and the Local Chief Executive mainly for
DRRM activities for the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF within
the five years preceding the current year.

35.3 We have recommended that the:
35.3.1 Municipal Treasurer deposit the unspent LDRRMF as of
December 31, 2020 amounting ₱3,760,308.00 to properly account
the fund and avoid possible mismanagement of the fund; and
35.3.2 Municipal Accountant record the journal entries to transfer the
unexpended LDRRMF from the GF to the TF upon deposit of the
unspent LDRRMF to the special trust fund account to enable
proper monitoring.

37.6 We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:
37.6.1 Submit the CY 2020 DILG-endorsed GPB;
37.6.2 Ensure gender mainstreaming in the Municipality through
integration of gender perspective in every programs, projects and
activities planned;
37.6.3 Ensure that GPB for succeeding years are in the correct form and
submitted to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office in accordance
with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2016-01 to ascertain GAD-responsiveness of the
programs; and
37.6.4 Submit supplemental GPB, if any.

Report together with the supporting reports and schedules.

HGDG which would be the basis in the budget determination depending
on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

guidelines or adopt DSWD policies on the grant of financial
assistance under the AICS program and other social programs of
the Municipality;

Annex for the paid AICS; and

supporting AICS are present before payments are made.

and proper implementation of the LCPC Fund to cater the needs and
concerns of the youth/ children within the locality.

We recommended that the:

observations.recommendation:

 That cash advances be granted, utilized and liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. For this, make sure that cash advances are granted only to authorized officers, properly utilized and liquidated accordingly; and

observations.managementAction: Management will consider request for write-off after demand letter is sent to the debtor. Submission of the liquidation reports and documents has improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS to serve as benchmark for collection effectiveness and efficiency measures; and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate and reconcile their records with the Municipal Assessor to establish correctness of collectible real property taxes and determine any changes/updates in the real properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all documentary requirements supporting all procurement and disbursements are present; and that disbursements that are not properly documented should be discontinued.

advances are granted only to authorized officers, properly utilized
and liquidated accordingly; and

monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation reports
and supporting documents.

cash advance upon submission of the Liquidation; and

payment is possible to prevent losses and provide reasonable
assurance that the transaction is regular.

ledgers for all payables to avoid over and under remittances; and

promptly remitted to the concerned agencies to avoid imposition of
penalties and inconveniences to employee-members.

10.18.1.2 Trace the beginning balances of the accounts and

10.18.1.3 Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the

Item 5 (d) of P.D. 1445.

15.15.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all

regular.
